behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "request sent to the external API always includes the complete list". Slot B: handoff boundary (request-builder -> API client payload). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a test of only the prompt-construction function can miss wiring bugs where dispatch drops/overwrites labels. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (stub client is enough), no dependence on model output content, no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text focuses on request payload inclusion, not live provider behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake API transport that captures outbound payload. Call classify(document, taxonomy) and assert the captured request contains every taxonomy label exactly as configured at dispatch time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local mapping/validation logic from provider label to allowed taxonomy. No module handoff is required to reproduce; no real infra; no model-output-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/validation function with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and raw label "medical"; assert result is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is determinism of one local normalization step. Real failure is in pure function behavior, not in inter-module wiring or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-test) with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical output label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "repeated live API calls" and consistency unless provider response changes. Slot B: model-output boundary (real AI API non-determinism). Slot C: if lowered to System, a deterministic stub/fixture can always return consistent labels and hide drift/variance from real model behavior. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this is repeated single-step calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls against the provider for the same input/taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label changes occur only when raw provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "before dispatch" budget enforcement. Slot B: handoff boundary (token-budget policy component -> request dispatcher). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, policy logic can pass while integration still dispatches untrimmed payload due to wiring/order bugs. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no correctness dependence on live model output; no multi-step agentic sequence. Rejection-gate: System rejected because a fake transport can still prove pre-dispatch enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, inject fake tokenizer + fake API client capture, call classify on oversized document, and assert dispatch receives either truncated payload or no dispatch occurs (reject path), per configured policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Provider-reported token usage" within budget. Slot B: model-output/real AI API boundary (usage comes from actual provider execution). Slot C: if lowered to System/Integration with mocks, fixed fake usage values can always satisfy budget and miss real provider tokenization/accounting differences. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this checks one model call outcome metric, not chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real provider calls for representative documents and taxonomy prompt, read usage tokens from provider response metadata, and assert each call's reported usage is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "API signals rate too high" then waits/retries. Slot B: handoff boundary across retry policy, scheduler/wait logic, and API client state transitions. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated retry helper tests can miss that classifier integration still surfaces first 429 without invoking retry loop. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required (scripted fake client responses and controllable clock), no dependence on model output quality, no multi-step agentic planning chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because real network timing is not required to prove retry behavior correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns rate-limit error once (or twice) then success, with a controllable clock/timer; assert classify waits per backoff policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of propagating rate-limit error.
